mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending power, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following INDPRO increases, or the signal reverses direction during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a relatively low federal funds rate compresses real yields and forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 60-day returns in >58% of instances, or the predictive IC drops below 0.01 when CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms economic expansion and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >62% of observations, or the signal shows zero correlation with equity returns during financial stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.5 percent signals economic stagnation and weak aggregate demand, prompting immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in >65% of periods when GDPC1 growth falls below the threshold, or the signal loses predictive accuracy after controlling for monetary policy expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production alongside stable consumer prices indicates healthy economic growth without inflationary overheating, which sustains equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative 20-day returns in >57% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL volatility exceeds historical norms.
